description: TypeScript compatibility is structural, so extra-compatible shapes can still drift semantically.
body:
Matching property shapes does not prove semantic compatibility. Public APIs still need tests for meaning, defaults, and runtime behavior.

Evidence boundary: apply only when current task, repository, or validation evidence matches this pack boundary. Do not override a more specific pack or explicit project contract. Use when TypeScript / JavaScript work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
